reason: The single residual finding is a minor omission of a disclaimer note clarifying that the code example is intentionally incomplete. The core technical content (doclet configuration, required paths, param settings, and the full working example) is fully captured. Users can apply the tool correctly without this note. The omission may cause mild confusion for users who wonder why the example lacks standard Javadoc elements, but this is unlikely to block task completion.

findings_assessment.justification: The omitted text is a qualifier note stating the example shows only tool-specific settings and that users should refer to Ant Javadoc Task docs for other settings. The knowledge file already includes the complete tool-specific configuration (doclet class, path setup, -output and -tag params) and the full working XML example. A user following the knowledge file can correctly configure and run the tool. The missing note would reduce confusion for users unfamiliar with Ant Javadoc task configuration (who might try to copy the minimal example verbatim), but this is a marginal use case. The finding persisted because the Phase E output is identical to the Phase D state — no edit was actually applied to the knowledge file.
